ask_next.question: Is the person breathing at all — even gasping occasionally?
ask_next.purpose: Confirm CPR indication and identify agonal respirations
ask_next.why: Agonal gasps (occasional gasps) are not normal breathing — CPR should continue

response_guides.note: Not breathing or agonal gasps — continue CPR without pause
Person has resumed breathing — stop compressions, place in recovery position, monitor closely

ask_next.question: Is there an AED (automated defibrillator) available nearby?
ask_next.why: Early defibrillation within 3-5 minutes dramatically improves survival

response_guides.note: Use AED as soon as it arrives — apply pads, follow prompts, resume CPR immediately after shock
No AED available — continue CPR without interruption until EMS arrives

ask_next.question: Do you know what caused them to collapse?
ask_next.purpose: Identify traumatic vs. cardiac arrest to adjust technique
ask_next.why: Traumatic arrest requires minimal head tilt; cardiac arrest benefits from standard protocol

response_guides.note: Traumatic arrest — minimize cervical spine movement; use jaw thrust instead of head tilt if possible
Drowning cardiac arrest — give 5 rescue breaths before starting compressions (modified protocol)
